thesis_summary: Training and inference workloads continue to require more accelerator capacity across hyperscalers enterprises and AI labs.
key_drivers: Model scaling demand; inference adoption; cloud capex competition
risks: Customer digestion cycles; ASIC substitution; export controls

thesis_title: Packaging capacity is a key AI supply bottleneck
thesis_summary: Advanced packaging remains critical because leading AI accelerators need high bandwidth memory and complex chiplet integration.
key_drivers: CoWoS demand; HBM attach rates; foundry investment
risks: Capacity additions overshooting demand; yield constraints

thesis_title: HBM is becoming a strategic memory profit pool
thesis_summary: AI accelerators need increasing HBM content which can improve memory supplier mix and margins.
key_drivers: Higher HBM stacks; accelerator launches; tight supply

thesis_title: Power availability constrains AI data center growth
thesis_summary: High density AI clusters increase demand for power distribution thermal management and backup power infrastructure.
key_drivers: Rack density growth; grid constraints; data center backlog
risks: Project delays; customer capex pauses; competition

thesis_summary: AI training clusters require high performance switching interconnect and software stacks to keep accelerators utilized.
key_drivers: Scale-out architectures; Ethernet adoption; bundled platforms

thesis_title: Custom AI silicon will coexist with GPUs
thesis_summary: Large cloud customers will use ASICs for cost and workload optimization while GPUs remain flexible general purpose platforms.
key_drivers: Hyperscaler scale; inference optimization; supply diversification
risks: Design concentration; software friction; lower merchant TAM
